headline_drivers: Q1 2026 results: revenue of $8.1M vs ~$7.0M consensus and adjusted EPS loss of $0.01 vs -$0.04 expected, plus record ~$15M in bookings and record free cash flow, highlighting a strong backlog and balance sheet (earnings release and coverage around 2026-05-06/07)
Maintained 2026 guidance for 20–25% revenue growth, ~40% gross margin, and positive EBITDA despite macro and supply‑chain headwinds, reinforcing a multi‑quarter growth narrative (2026-05-06/07)

Earlier strategic repositioning: sale of Bressner Technology GmbH for ~$22.4M and focus on becoming a ‘pure play leader in ruggedized AI, ML and edge compute solutions,’ leaving OSS with roughly $34–40M net cash and >20% growth / >40% GM core profile, prompting analysts like Roth/MKM to raise price targets (2025-11 to 2026-02 window)

$10.5M in new U.S. Navy / defense awards plus a $1.2M Safran Federal Systems order, reinforcing a growing, higher‑margin defense and ruggedized AI compute franchise that supports the re‑rating (February–March 2026)
summary: The stock’s sharp move is being driven primarily by a very strong Q1 2026 print and call: OSS beat on revenue and EPS, posted record ~$15M bookings and record free cash flow, and reiterated 2026 guidance for 20–25% revenue growth, ~40% gross margins, and positive EBITDA, all while highlighting a robust backlog and cash position (~$34M+). That earnings beat is landing on top of a multi‑month strategic re‑rating—divesting the lower‑growth Bressner business, amassing ~$40M net cash, and repositioning as a focused ruggedized AI/edge compute and defense contractor with $10.5M+ in recent Navy and other defense awards. Together, this combination of cleaner story, visible growth, and balance‑sheet strength has triggered momentum buying and an aggressive multiple expansion in a small‑cap name, which explains the 5–20 day surge far more than technicals alone.
